SALES PAGE – QUICKstart GUIDE
Launch: THE SALES PAGE
OVERVIEW
When you’re ready to open your shopping cart and start taking orders, you have to have a
strong sales page. Even though the people who have been through your Launch Sequence
have already been pre-sold, it’s important not to skimp on your sales message.
The number one thing to remember about your sales message is that your offer is not about
the stuff, it’s about the transformation. It’s about how your prospect’s life is going to be
transformed. Your product is how they get the transformation, but your sales video or sales
letter has to communicate the transformation, then ask for the money.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 6 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
SALES PAGE – QUICKstart GUIDE
Developing your Sales Message
To help you focus your message on the transformation, think about the following as you
write your offer:
What is the biggest problem or struggle your prospect has?
Why are they having this problem?
What is the problem costing them?
Why can’t they solve the problem on their own?
What are the most common mistakes people make trying to solve the problem?
What are the biggest misconceptions about the problem?
How would someone fix or solve the problem?
How will they benefit from solving the problem (how will it make their life better)?
Remember, the whole focus is around them and what their problem is and how you can
solve it for them. You need to describe their problem better than they can for them to believe
you can solve it for them.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
SALES PAGE – QUICKstart GUIDE
The Framework
Here’s a formula for creating your sales video or sales letter:
• 	Headline: Describe the big benefit.
• 	Sub-Headline: Describe the problem and main benefit of the solution.
• 	Sales Message (this works for a sales video or sales letter):
o 	The big promise or hook: “What’s in it for me?”
o 	The opportunity and transformation
o 	Show authority (especially results)
o 	Raise and Answer Objections
o 	Proof and testimonials
o 	Foreshadow urgency
o 	Make your crushing offer: Cover the benefits and features.
§ 	Who is the product for?
§ 	What does it do?
§ 	What are all the ways your product will transform your prospect’s life for the
better?
§ 	What makes it different from other products in the market?
§ 	How does it work?
§ 	Who made or discovered it?
§ 	What are some interesting facts about it (stats, scientific proof, unique
characteristics)?
§ 	Why was it created in the first place?
§ 	What are the deliverables?
§ 	How and when do they get it?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
SALES PAGE – QUICKstart GUIDE
o 	Bonuses: Add bonuses and describe any scarcity with bonuses (launch bonuses, fast
mover bonuses for first 5 buyers, etc.).
o 	Price reveal
§ 	Price comparison/justification
§ 	Payment methods
§ 	Payment plan details (if offered)
o 	Here’s what to do now: Describe the process to actually buy from you.
o 	Urgency: Scarcity – why should they act NOW!
§ 	Quantity limit (100 seats available)
§ 	Physical limit (100 copies available)
§ 	Workload limit (20 clients at a time)
§ 	Fast action bonus (1st 10 customers get….)
§ 	Viewing limit (only see special offer page once)
§ 	Schedule limit (class starts at 8pm)
§ 	Deadline (registration closes at midnight)
o 	Call to action/close: For your Buy Button, test button text options like “add to cart”,
“join now”, etc.
o 	Risk reversal: Guarantee – 30-day, 60-day, 90-day?
o 	Surprise bonus
o 	Re-close (second call to action) with scarcity
o 	P.S. with social proof: Testimonials, blog comments, number of people who bought,
etc.
o 	Final call to action

SALES PAGE – QUICKstart GUIDE
SALES PAGE CHECKLIST
¨ Did you answer the key transformation questions before writing your sales message
(and include those elements in your sales message)?
¨ Did you test your headline and sub-headline to find the one that grabs your
prospect’s attention?
¨ Did you start with a big promise?
¨ Did you tell a story of transformation and results?
¨ Did you include testimonials and proof?
¨ Did you clearly review the benefits?
¨ Did you cover all of the features with a focus on transformation?
¨ Did you include multiple formats for your deliverables such as print (ebook), audio
(MP3), video, etc.?
¨ Did you include 3-5 bonus items?
¨ Did you juxtapose/compare the price against the value provided?
¨ For videos:
o 	Did you hide the buy button on the page until you got to the point in the video
where you made your call to action?
o 	Did you review your delivery? (Do you sound authentic, are you looking at the
camera without staring at it, is the energy engaging and appropriate?)
¨ Did you provide a clear call to action and reclose again at the end?
¨ Did you include urgency and scarcity in your offer?
¨ Did you include social proof?
¨ Did you remove the risk by providing a guarantee?
¨ Did you test different options for the text on your buy button?
¨ Did you test your sales page on multiple browsers and mobile devices?
¨ Did you test your checkout process and product delivery?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
